risks: AKS serviceCidr (10.2.0.0/16) overlaps with VNet address space (10.0.0.0/14, which covers 10.0.0.0–10.3.255.255) — this will cause AKS cluster creation to fail
ACR has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled, exposing container images to the public internet
Service Bus Standard tier uses shared capacity with variable latency, which may not meet SLA requirements for payment processing
Single-region deployment (eastus only) with no cross-region disaster recovery strategy
No explicit per-service database isolation for order and payment services — only catalog data store (Cosmos DB) is defined
Redis Standard SKU lacks VNet injection — cache traffic is not isolated within the virtual network
No NSG or Azure Firewall resources defined for subnet-level traffic filtering
correctionsRecommended: Change AKS serviceCidr to a range outside the VNet address space (e.g., 172.16.0.0/16) to eliminate the CIDR overlap
Add private endpoint resources for ACR, Cosmos DB, Key Vault, and Service Bus on snet-services-001 to enforce private connectivity
Add Cosmos DB database and container child resources for order and payment services, or document the shared-database strategy
Update Cosmos DB dependency from vnet-ecommerce-prod-eastus-001 to snet-services-001 where service endpoints are actually configured
Add kv-ecommerce-prod-001 to AKS dependencies since the cluster uses the Key Vault CSI driver addon
Consider upgrading Redis to Premium SKU to enable VNet injection for network-isolated caching
Add Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups resources and associate them with both subnets
hardDependencies: AKS serviceCidr (10.2.0.0/16) overlaps with VNet CIDR (10.0.0.0/14) — Azure will reject the managed cluster creation
